rationale: The postcondition requires post1_self_.epmap == post2_self_.epmap (struct equality on HashMap<CAckState>). HashMap<V> is #[verifier(external_body)] without #[verifier::ext_equal], so spec struct equality is uninterpreted by Verus and not implied by view equality. The spec swap_spec only constrains the view (via put_spec: new_map_v == old_map_v.insert(key, value)), so view equality between post1_self_.epmap@ and post2_self_.epmap@ IS derivable, but lifting that to struct equality on .epmap requires an axiom we cannot legally add (no assume / external_body / assume_specification in proof block). All other parts of the equality (r1==r2 unit type, post1_ack_state struct equality, num_packets_acked, un_acked) ARE derivable; tried the direct assertion `assert(post1_self_.epmap == post2_self_.epmap)` and it failed with `assertion failed`, confirming the gap is fundamental rather than a trigger issue.
